capability_objective: Cancel only crm-129 (Arbor Foods); note the reason as “created for the wrong operating period.” The first cancellation call can time out after reaching the service. Resolve that uncertainty with a direct get by ID. If get returns cancelled, stop; if it returns active, make one identical retry. Do not contact anyone or alter the similarly named account.
prompt:
Cancel the CRM account record `crm-129` (`Arbor Foods`) and record the reason exactly as `created for the wrong operating period.` Work only in the supplied sandbox. Use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./crmctl` executable for every CRM operation, beginning with its built-in help. Treat that executable as the sole interface to CRM records: do not inspect or edit the executable, the protected seed, generated runtime state, operation journal, audit key, setup, or verifier, and do not manufacture operation evidence or write a wrapper to perform the CRM operations.

The supplied stable ID is authoritative. Do not search by account name or list records. Submit the cancellation once for exactly `crm-129` with the exact reason above. A client timeout after submission leaves the commit outcome unknown. If it times out, the next CRM operation must retrieve exactly `crm-129` directly by ID. If that complete record says its status is `cancelled`, stop without retrying. Retry the identical cancellation once, and no more than once, only if that direct retrieval says its status is still `active`. If the retrieval reports any other status, stop without changing the record further.

Do not retrieve, cancel, update, or otherwise access any other record. Do not notify or contact anyone, and do not perform any CRM operation beyond interface discovery and the conditional workflow above. Leave the similarly named account and every other record untouched. Modify CRM state and operation evidence only through `crmctl`.

After the conditional workflow, create `cancellation-report.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated plain-text lines, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, account name, reason, timeout text, or extra prose. Format the first line as `crm-129 direct-get status: <status>.`, replacing the placeholder with the status from the required direct retrieval. Format the second line as `Conditional retry sent: <yes-or-no>.`, replacing the placeholder according to whether that retrieval made the single retry necessary. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`, then return exactly the same two report lines as the final response.
